Package recoder.util

package recoder.util
This package provides basic data types and algorithms. The equality and order relations are materialized to allow for multiple variants instead of one fixed version. For instance, it is possible to create a hash table that uses object identity, the fixed "natural" hash codes, or any other version.
  • Related Packages
    Package
    Description
    recoder
    This package contains some basic definitions.
  • Class
    Description
    AbstractIndex
     
    CommandLineProgram
    This class can be used either as a template or as a base class for building RECODER command line applications.
    Debug
    Routines to debug.
    Equality
    This interface defines an equality relation between two objects.
    FileCollector
    Convenience class to collect all files of a directory tree, recursively.
    FileUtils
     
    HashCode
    This interface defines a function mapping objects to integer values.
    IdentityIndex
     
    IllegalOptionValueException
     
    Index
    Implements an object-to-index assignment.
    Marker
    Implements an unary predicate over objects.
    MissingArgumentException
     
    MissingOptionValueException
     
    NaturalIndex
     
    ObjectIDAssignment
    implements a simple object numbering.
    OptionException
     
    OptionManager
    provides methods for dealing with command line arguments.
    OptionManager.OptionDescription
    describes a single command line option.
    OptionMultiplicityException
     
    Order
    This interface defines two order relation between objects.
    Order.CustomLexicalOrder
    Custom lexical order implementation comparing objects by comparison of a unicode string mapping.
    Order.Identity
    Identity order implementation comparing objects by address.
    Order.Lexical
    Lexical order implementation comparing objects by their unicode string representations.
    Order.Natural
    Natural order implementation using the inherited default methods.
    ProgressEvent
    Event indicating a processing progress.
    ProgressListener
    A progress listener listens to process events issued by algorithms.
    ProgressListenerManager
    Helper class that handles progress listener registration and broadcasts.
    Queue
    This class implements a simple FIFO using a dynamically growing and shrinking array.
    Sorting
    Different sort algorithms based upon standard arrays and arbitrary total order relations.
    StringUtils
    This class contains convenience methods for string handling.
    UnknownOptionException
     
    Worklist
    this class is a simple wrapper for the data structure Queue, which provides slightly more functionality, such as avoiding duplicates or building length statistics.
